entities.name: EXTRA-SHORT-DURATION PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A relatively new plant type bred to replace short-duration pigeonpea for cultivation in rotation with wheat in the western Indo-Gangetic plain

entities.name: SHORT-DURATION PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A pigeonpea variety used in rotation with wheat, which is being replaced by extra-short-duration varieties

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A crop grown in rotation with pigeonpea in the Indo-Gangetic plain

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A crop mentioned as a comparison for the yield of the following wheat crop

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: The characteristic of maturing two to four weeks earlier than short-duration cultivars

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of the crop per unit area

entities.name: ON-FARM TESTING
entities.description: The process used to evaluate the performance of crop rotations in farmers' fields

entities.name: INDO-GANGETIC PLAIN
entities.description: The geographical region in South Asia where the crop rotation is practiced

entities.name: NORTHWESTERN INDIA
entities.description: The specific region where on-farm testing was conducted, including Sonepat, Ghaziabad, and Ludhiana

entities.name: ABIOTIC AND BIOTIC CONSTRAINTS
entities.description: Environmental and biological factors that limit the realization of potential yield

entities.name: SOCIOECONOMIC CONSTRAINTS
entities.description: Social and economic factors that limit the widespread adoption of extra-short-duration pigeonpea

entities.name: SEED PRODUCTION AND STORAGE
entities.description: Practices and precautions necessary for maintaining seed quality and quantity

relationships.description: compared preceding crop effect on yield
